name: Functional Equations — Solving for a Whole Function
whatIsIt: A FUNCTIONAL EQUATION gives a rule that a mystery function f must obey for ALL inputs, and asks you to find every f that works. The classic is Cauchy's equation f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), whose nice solutions are f(x) = cx. The main tools are SUBSTITUTION (plug in clever values like x = 0, y = 0, y = x) to squeeze out facts such as f(0) and whether f is odd or even.
whyNeeded: Instead of solving for a number, you solve for an entire function — a big step up in thinking. Substitution tricks here train the exact reasoning Olympiads reward and that underlies proofs across algebra.
realLifeUses: Describing processes where combining inputs adds outputs (scaling laws)
Modelling growth where f(x+y) relates to f(x) and f(y)
Deriving formulas that must hold for every value, not just one
Checking that a proposed formula is the only one that fits

vocabulary.meaning: An equation that a function must satisfy for all inputs.
Plugging in special values (0, x, −x) to reveal properties of f.
f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y); its well-behaved solutions are f(x) = cx.

story.title: The Mystery Machine
story.text: Anaya finds a machine f with a note: f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) for all numbers. 'What is f(0)?' asks the Professor. She sets x = 0, y = 0: f(0) = f(0) + f(0), so f(0) = 0! 'Now set y = −x,' he says. f(0) = f(x) + f(−x) = 0, so f(−x) = −f(x) — it's odd. 'And f(1) = c fixes everything: f(x) = cx.' Anaya beams: 'Clever substitutions unlocked the whole machine.'
story.extractedProblem: If f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) for all x, y, what is f(0)?
story.answerInStory: 0 — set x = y = 0 to get f(0) = 2f(0), so f(0) = 0.

visual.caption: The well-behaved solutions of Cauchy's equation are straight lines through the origin, f(x) = cx. Substitution proves f(0) = 0 and f(−x) = −f(x).

standardMethod.summary: Attack a functional equation by substituting special inputs: x = 0 and y = 0 to find f(0); y = x to relate f(2x) and f(x); y = −x to test odd/even. Combine the facts to pin down the formula, then check it satisfies the original.
steps: Substitute x = 0 (and y = 0) to find f(0).
Substitute y = −x to test whether f is odd or even.
Substitute y = x to relate f(2x) to f(x).
Guess the family (often f(x) = cx) and verify it fits for all inputs.

workedExamples.problem: Given f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) for all x, y, find f(0).
If also f(1) = 3 and f(x) = cx, find f(4).

commonMistakes.mistake: Solving for a single input instead of all inputs.
commonMistakes.fix: A functional equation must hold for EVERY input — find the whole function, not one value.

commonMistakes.mistake: Guessing a formula but never checking it in the original.
commonMistakes.fix: Always substitute your candidate back to confirm it satisfies the equation for all x, y.

easy.q: If f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y) for all x, y, then f(0) = ?
The nice solutions of f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) look like…

medium.q: f(x) = cx with f(1) = 5. Find f(3).

challenge.q: f(x) = cx with f(2) = 10. Find f(5).
If f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) and f(1)=4, find f(0)+f(3).

questions.q: The trick that finds f(0) is to substitute…

options.label: Check it in the original equation

questions.answer: Check it in the original equation

teachBackPrompt: Show a grown-up the machine f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y). Set x = y = 0 to prove f(0) = 0, then y = −x to prove f(−x) = −f(x). Explain how clever substitution reveals the whole function f(x) = cx.

revisionCard.summary: Functional equation: find every f obeying a rule for ALL inputs. Tools: substitute x=0, y=0 (find f(0)); y=−x (odd/even); y=x (relate f(2x)). Cauchy f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) ⇒ f(x)=cx. Always verify your candidate.

rememberIt.hook: Substitute 0, then y = −x — the function reveals itself
rememberIt.unpack: f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) ⇒ f(0)=0, f odd, f(x)=cx. Verify at the end.

studentQuestions.q: Are there weird solutions to Cauchy's equation?
studentQuestions.a: Yes, exotic non-linear ones exist in theory, but with any mild niceness (continuity, or holding on the rationals) the only solutions are f(x) = cx.

studentQuestions.q: How do I know which substitution to make?
studentQuestions.a: Aim to simplify: zeros kill terms, y = −x tests symmetry, y = x doubles the input. Try the substitution that removes the most clutter.

formulas.name: Cauchy's equation
formulas.formula: f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) ⇒ f(x) = cx
